title: Discounts and instalments
instruction: Answer these English and maths questions about real-life situations.

questions.question: A sofa costs £1,200. The shop offers '12 monthly instalments'. What does 'instalment' mean?
options: One of a series of regular payments
questions.answer: One of a series of regular payments

questions.question: A label says 'mortgage interest rate: 4.5% p.a.' What does 'p.a.' mean?
Which sentence correctly uses a fraction greater than 1?
options: I spent 5/3 of my budget — less than planned
I spent 5/3 of my budget — more than planned
questions.answer: I spent 5/3 of my budget — more than planned

questions.question: A laptop costs £840. It is reduced by 1/3. What is the sale price?
You pay for a £960 sofa in 12 equal monthly instalments. How much is each payment?
A mortgage of £150,000 has 4.5% annual interest. How much interest do you pay in the first year?
